name: Microwave Artichokes
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the stem from each artichoke and cut off the base using a sharp knife so they sit flat.
2. Make a diagonal cut across the top of each leaf, parallel to the base. Use scissors to trim the leaves for straight edges.
3. For conventional cooking: Bring a large shallow pan of salted water to a boil. Submerge the artichokes and cover with a heat-proof heavy plate to keep them submerged. Cook for 35 to 45 minutes or until a leaf can be pulled out easily. Drain upside down to let excess water run through.
4. For microwave cooking: Wrap each artichoke individually in microwavable plastic wrap and place them in a round dish. Microwave on high for 10 minutes or until a center leaf can be pulled out easily. Carefully remove the plastic wrap, as it will be hot.
5. Serve immediately, allowing each person to pull out a leaf and dip it in melted butter. Optionally, reserve a cone of dinner leaves for later, scoop out the choke with a spoon, replace the cone upside down, and serve with lemon-butter or vinaigrette.
Yield: 2 servings
